inline_comments.message: Why do you imply that this job doesn't exists when it's defined in jjb/global-templates-java.yaml? According to it's synopsis what it does is "# Job template for Java daily release jobs running checkstyle at the end"
What I actually didn't find is this job definition, where is it defined?

How is that global template applied and where are those jobs, if anyone actually uses that definition? https://jenkins.onap.org/view/all-jobs/ doesn't have any *-daily-checkstyle jobs, nor are there any such jobs visible under, say, https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk/ for ccsdk either.

...well, it's there in Jenkins: https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk/job/ccsdk-distribution-maven-docker-stage-master/

As to what jjb generates this, your guess is probably better than mine. Maybe some jjb magic spins it from ci-management/jjb/ccsdk/distribution.yaml section

      - gerrit-maven-docker-stage:
          build-node: ubuntu1804-docker-8c-8g
          container-public-registry: 'nexus3.onap.org:10001'
          container-staging-registry: 'nexus3.onap.org:10003'
          mvn-params: '-Dmaven.test.skip=true -P docker'
          maven-versions-plugin: true
          cron: '@daily'
 
??
I based my comment just by looking at configuration, now checked with jenkins-jobs and ccsdk-master-release-java-daily-checkstyle doesn't seem to be created indeed.

Just checked it with JJB tool and indeed the job gets created although I can't see the template for this. What you're pointing at (distribution.yaml) are job definitions, not templates, so it's not there.
